story.iWant: to build all remaining slides at once with `/build-all`
story.soThat: I can generate my entire deck quickly without manually running /build-one for each slide

tasks.task: Implement Batch Build Prerequisites Check - 1.1: Read status.yaml to verify mode == "deck" - 1.2: If mode != "deck", return error: "/build-all is for deck mode only" - 1.3: Read deck/plan.yaml to get slides array - 1.4: Count slides with status: "pending" - 1.5: If zero pending slides, return: "All slides already built! Run /export when ready." - 1.6: Display: "Building {n} remaining slides..."

Implement Batch Generation Loop - 2.1: Iterate through slides array in order (by slide number) - 2.2: For each slide with status "pending": Display progress, Extract slide context, Determine template vs custom - 2.3: Reuse template decision logic from Story 5.3

Implement Per-Slide Generation - 3.1: For template-based slides: Load template, inject content, apply theme CSS - 3.2: For custom slides: Invoke frontend-design skill with full context - 3.3: Ensure all generated slides follow HTML Slide Pattern (1920x1080, contenteditable, data-field)

Implement Per-Slide Save Operations - 4.1: Save slide to deck/slides/slide-{n}.html - 4.2: Create empty state file: deck/slides/slide-{n}-state.json - 4.3: State file schema: { "slide": n, "edits": [], "lastModified": null }

Implement Error Handling - 5.1: Wrap each slide generation in try-catch - 5.2: On error: Log error message to console - 5.3: On error: Mark slide status as "failed" in plan.yaml - 5.4: On error: Continue to next slide (DO NOT abort batch) - 5.5: Track failed slide numbers for summary

Implement Progress Tracking - 6.1: After each successful slide, update plan.yaml: status → "built" - 6.2: Increment built_count tracking variable - 6.3: After batch completion, update status.yaml: built_count, last_action, last_modified - 6.4: Add entry to status.yaml history array

Implement Batch Summary - 7.1: Calculate summary stats: total attempted, successfully built, failed (with slide numbers) - 7.2: Display completion message: "Build complete! Built: {n}/{total} slides" - 7.3: If failures exist, add: "Failed slides: {list} - Retry failed slides with /build-one" - 7.4: Suggest next steps: "Review slides in deck/slides/", "Use /edit {n} to refine", "Run /export when ready"

Enhance Build Workflow Instructions - 8.1: Review .slide-builder/workflows/build/workflow.yaml - 8.2: Update/create .slide-builder/workflows/build-all/workflow.yaml if separate workflow - 8.3: Add batch mode phases to instructions.md

Register/Update Slash Command - 9.1: Create/update .claude/commands/sb/build-all.md - 9.2: Document deck mode requirement - 9.3: Document error continuation behavior - 9.4: Include example output in command docs

Testing - Batch Build Verification - 10.1-10.7: Full batch build test (7 slides, all pending → all built) - 10.8-10.10: Error scenario test (continue on failure, mark failed) - 10.11: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given a deck plan exists with unbuilt slides, when the user runs `/build-all`, then the system iterates through all unbuilt slides in order
Each slide is generated using template or frontend-design skill based on intent
Each slide is saved to `.slide-builder/deck/slides/slide-{n}.html` with corresponding state file
Progress is reported during generation (e.g., "Building slide 3 of 7...")
plan.yaml statuses are updated as each slide completes (status: "built")
If an error occurs during batch generation, the error is logged and generation continues with the next slide
Failed slides are marked in plan.yaml with status: "failed" for retry
Final summary shows total slides built and any failures (e.g., "Build complete: 7/8 slides. Failed: slide 3")
status.yaml is updated with final built_count and batch action in history

doc.name: tech-spec-epic-5.md
doc.summary: Epic 5 technical specification with Batch Builder module design, /build-all workflow phases, data models (plan.yaml schema, slide status enum), and complete workflow diagrams

relevantSections.section: Services and Modules - Batch Builder module definition
Data Models and Contracts - plan.yaml schema, slide status enum (pending/built/failed)
Workflows and Sequencing - Complete /build-all Workflow (3 phases)

doc.name: architecture.md
doc.summary: System architecture defining BMAD-pattern structure, Novel Patterns (Template-or-Custom Decision, Dual-Mode State Management), HTML Slide Pattern, and State File Patterns

relevantSections.section: Novel Pattern 2: Template-or-Custom Decision - Intent keyword → template mapping
Novel Pattern 4: Dual-Mode State Management - Mode detection from status.yaml
Implementation Patterns - HTML Slide Pattern (contenteditable, data-field, 1920x1080)
State File Patterns - status.yaml schema (mode, built_count, history)

doc.name: prd.md
doc.summary: Product requirements document with FR22-30 (Building), FR48-52 (Framework), and NFR definitions

relevantSections.section: NFR14: Partial deck builds are recoverable
NFR17: State files are human-readable YAML

file.name: build-all.md
file.summary: Slash command definition for /sb:build-all - currently minimal, needs enhancement
file.content:
--- description: 'Build all remaining slides in deck plan' ---
# Slide Builder - Build All Command
This command builds all remaining slides in your deck plan.
<steps CRITICAL="TRUE"> 1. Read the workflow configuration at @.slide-builder/workflows/build/workflow.yaml 2. Read the instructions at @.slide-builder/workflows/build/instructions.md 3. Verify mode is "deck" from .slide-builder/status.yaml 4. Iterate through all unbuilt slides and generate HTML 5. Update .slide-builder/status.yaml with workflow progress </steps>
file.notes: This command needs enhancement to implement batch mode phases, error continuation, and summary reporting per ACs

file.name: build-one.md
file.summary: Slash command for /sb:build-one - single slide build with mode detection
file.content:
--- description: 'Build the next slide (or single planned slide) from plan using theme' ---
# Slide Builder - Build One Command
**Behavior depends on current mode:** - **Single mode:** Builds the slide from `.slide-builder/single/plan.yaml` - **Deck mode:** Builds the next unbuilt slide from `.slide-builder/deck/plan.yaml`
<steps CRITICAL="TRUE"> 1. Read the workflow configuration at @.slide-builder/workflows/build-one/workflow.yaml 2. Read the instructions at @.slide-builder/workflows/build-one/instructions.md 3. Execute the 5-phase workflow: - Phase 1: Mode Detection & Routing (check status.yaml mode) - Phase 1B: Deck Build Router (find next pending slide in deck plan) - Phase 2: Template Decision (map template to sample file or use custom) - Phase 3/3B: Template or Custom Build (generate slide HTML) - Phase 4: Save Slide (create .html and state.json files) - Phase 5: Update Progress (update plan.yaml status, status.yaml counts) 4. All text elements must have contenteditable="true" and data-field attributes 5. Slide must render at 1920x1080 (16:9) 6. In deck mode, create corresponding slide-{n}-state.json for Epic 4 compatibility </steps>
file.notes: Story 5.4 batch build reuses this per-slide logic - the Batch Builder wraps this in a loop

file.name: workflow.yaml
file.summary: Build-one workflow configuration with template mapping and intent keywords
file.relevantContent:
name: build-one description: "Build the next slide (or single planned slide) from plan using theme" version: "2.0"
# Template file mapping (suggested_template -> actual file) template_mapping: layout-title: "01-title.html" layout-list: "02-agenda.html" layout-flow: "03-flow.html" layout-columns-2: "04-comparison.html" layout-columns-3: "04-comparison.html" layout-callout: "05-callout.html" layout-code: "06-technical.html"
# Intent keyword to template mapping intent_keywords: layout-title: ["title", "intro", "opening", "cover"] layout-list: ["list", "bullets", "points", "agenda", "steps"] layout-flow: ["flow", "process", "timeline", "journey", "workflow"] layout-columns-2: ["compare", "vs", "two", "side-by-side", "ROI", "numbers", "metrics"] layout-columns-3: ["three", "triad", "options", "landscape", "products"] layout-callout: ["key", "insight", "callout", "cta", "important", "highlight", "exercise"] layout-code: ["code", "technical", "api", "commands", "CLI"]
# Slide status enum slide_status: ["pending", "built", "failed"]
# State file schema for deck slides state_file_schema: slide: "number" edits: "[]" lastModified: "null"
file.notes: Story 5.4 reuses these mappings for template decision in batch mode

file.name: instructions.md

keyPhases.phase: Mode Detection and Routing - read status.yaml, route to single or deck mode
Deck Build Router - find first pending slide from plan.yaml
Template Decision - map template name to sample file or fall back to custom
Template Build - load sample, inject content with theme CSS variables
Custom Build - invoke frontend-design skill with full context
Save Slide - write HTML file and empty state.json
Update Progress - update plan.yaml status, status.yaml built_count and history

criticalRequirements.requirement: All text elements MUST have contenteditable="true" attribute
All text elements MUST have unique data-field attribute
Google Fonts link for Outfit font

file.notes: Story 5.4 wraps Phases 1B through 5 in a loop with error continuation

file.name: status.yaml
file.currentState:
mode: deck current_slide: 2 total_slides: 10 built_count: 2
phase: complete workflow_version: "2.0"
theme: file: .slide-builder/theme.json status: locked personality: bold confidence: 0.85
deck: name: "Claude Code Fundamentals" plan_file: .slide-builder/deck/plan.yaml slides_dir: .slide-builder/deck/slides/
last_action: "Built slide 2: What is Claude Code?" last_modified: "2026-01-27T16:05:00Z"
file.notes: Batch build will update: built_count (increment per slide), last_action ("Batch built N slides"), history (append batch entry)

file.name: plan.yaml
file.summary: Current deck plan with 10 slides (2 built, 8 pending)
file.structure:
deck_name: "Claude Code Fundamentals" audience: "Solution Engineers" audience_knowledge_level: beginner
storyline: opening_hook: "SEs need to confidently demo AI coding capabilities..." tension: "Multiple Claude products exist..." resolution: "Claude Code is the agentic CLI..." call_to_action: "Install Claude Code today..."
slides: - number: 1, status: built, template: layout-title - number: 2, status: built, template: layout-callout - number: 3, status: pending, template: layout-callout - number: 4, status: pending, template: layout-columns-3 - number: 5, status: pending, template: layout-list - number: 6, status: pending, template: layout-list - number: 7, status: pending, template: layout-code - number: 8, status: pending, template: layout-flow - number: 9, status: pending, template: layout-callout - number: 10, status: pending, template: layout-columns-2
file.notes: Batch build will iterate pending slides 3-10, updating each status to "built" (or "failed" on error)

file.name: theme.json
file.summary: Brand theme primitives - Amperity brand with bold-minimal personality
file.keyValues:
colors: primary: "#CCFF00" (lime yellow) secondary: "#5DBAB6" (teal) accent: "#FF6B6B" (coral) background.dark: "#1A1A1A" text.onDark: "#FFFFFF"
typography: fonts.heading: "'Outfit', system-ui, sans-serif" scale.hero: "72px", scale.h1: "48px"
personality: classification: "bold-minimal" traits: ["tech-forward", "data-driven", "high-contrast"]
file.notes: All generated slides must inject these as CSS variables in :root

file.name: slide-1.html
file.summary: Example generated deck slide showing required HTML structure
file.keyPatterns: - Viewport: width=1920, height=1080 - Body/slide dimensions: 1920px x 1080px - All text elements have contenteditable="true" - All text elements have data-field attributes - Google Fonts link for Outfit - CSS variables from theme.json in :root - Auto-save script for contenteditable elements - data-slide-id attribute on .slide container
file.notes: All batch-generated slides must follow this exact pattern

file.name: 01-title.html
file.summary: Sample title slide template for reference
file.notes: Used as design reference when template: layout-title is specified in plan.yaml

dependencies.dependency: Used for Google Slides API in /export (Epic 7)
Invoked for custom slide generation when no template matches intent

constraints.constraint: Deck mode MUST use `.slide-builder/deck/` directory exclusively
Each slide MUST be saved as `deck/slides/slide-{n}.html` with matching state file
Batch builds MUST continue on error (don't abort entire batch)
Template selection MUST follow the intent-to-template mapping pattern from workflow.yaml
All slides MUST have contenteditable and data-field attributes for Epic 4 compatibility
Google Fonts link for Outfit font family
CSS variables from theme.json must be in :root
status.yaml must be updated with built_count, last_action, and history after batch completion
plan.yaml slide statuses must be updated: "pending" → "built" or "failed"

interface.name: /sb:build-all
interface.output: All pending slides generated to deck/slides/, summary displayed

interface.name: plan.yaml
interface.purpose: Get slides array, find pending slides, read slide context

interface.name: theme.json
interface.purpose: Get brand primitives for CSS variable injection

interface.name: samples/*.html
interface.purpose: Template design reference for layout generation

interface.name: slide-{n}-state.json
interface.purpose: Empty state file for Epic 4 edit preservation

interface.name: frontend-design
interface.purpose: Generate custom slides when no template matches intent

standards.standard: Frontend Test Gate pattern - manual browser testing checklist
All slides must render at 1920x1080 in browser
All text elements must be editable (contenteditable visible)
Theme colors and fonts must be applied correctly
Batch must continue past individual slide failures

locations.location: Frontend Test Gate checklist in story file (5-4-TG1)

ideas.testCase: Create plan with 7 pending slides, run /build-all, verify all 7 built
Verify progress messages: "Building slide 1 of 7...", "Building slide 2 of 7..."
Verify all slide-{n}.html files created in deck/slides/
Force one slide to fail (corrupt template), verify batch continues
Verify failed slide marked status: "failed" in plan.yaml
Verify summary shows "Built 6/7, Failed 1: slide X"
Mode not "deck": Error "/build-all is for deck mode only"
No pending slides: "All slides already built! Run /export when ready."
Partial pre-built: Only pending slides attempted, pre-built skipped
Verify generated slides have contenteditable and data-field attributes
Verify slides render at 1920x1080 in browser
